entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 31
entries.excerpt: When the sons of Laban too began to oppress Jacob, as Laban had oppressed him, and to say of him who had enriched them, that he had become rich from their father’s property; and when Laban himself, who earlier had confessed, “I have learned by experience that the Lord hath blessed me at thy coming” (Gen 30:27), changed toward Jacob both inwardly and outwardly — then God appeared to Jacob and said to him: “Return unto the land of thy father” (Gen 31:3).…
